family_a.note: a Write to a HEAD-committed binder is denied
a Write to a non-binder file passes
a Write to a committed archived binder is denied
a write via a symlink alias dir escapes BINDER_RE — the path is matched as the tool call names it, before any resolution
a binder staged but not committed passes the HEAD-only check (ls-tree HEAD misses the index)
malformed stdin fails open (exit 0) — binder guard must never break a call
a Write of an invalid pack is denied with validator findings
a Write of a validator-clean pack passes
a NotebookEdit writing an invalid pack passes — the live PreToolUse pack matcher is Write-only and the guard reads only tool_input.file_path
a recognized auditor dispatch without a binder path is denied (fail-closed)
a recognized dispatch naming an sme-pinned binder without checklist evidence is denied
a dispatch with binder path and checklist evidence passes
an unrecognized dispatch shape always passes
a kaizen write outside its surface is denied
a recognized kaizen write with unverifiable tool_input is denied (fail-closed on the recognized shape)
a Stop in a repo with a stranded built ref (no done/failed) is blocked, via the real refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/built namespace
an identical second Stop in the same session passes (block-once sentinel)
malformed stdin fails open (exit 0) — a stray Stop trap is worse than a missed nudge

rows.note: committed binders are read-only; Write matcher
committed binders are read-only; Edit matcher
committed binders are read-only; NotebookEdit matcher
seeded dark area: cat >, sed -i, git checkout -- of a binder are invisible to every Write|Edit|NotebookEdit matcher
a subagent's file writes surface as its own Write/Edit/NotebookEdit calls, which the same matchers see
bundled Codex twin ships in 2.24.0 (raw apply_patch parsing) but Codex trust-gates every bundled hook per definition — until the user reviews and trusts it, doctrine plus sandbox only
plain-terminal edits run zero guards — field evidence: gringotts 1f548e5
pre-land validation from proposed content; PostToolUse re-checks on disk
post-only and advisory — exit 2 feeds findings back, but a broken pack persists on disk until repaired (dark area: advisory correction)
seeded dark area: PreToolUse pack matcher is Write-only and the guard reads only tool_input.file_path — a NotebookEdit on a pack is never validated
seeded dark area: sed -i on a pack is invisible to the file-tool matchers
subagent pack writes surface as file-tool calls the matchers see
bundled Codex twin ships in 2.24.0 but is trust-gated per hook definition — until reviewed and trusted, doctrine plus OS sandbox only
plain-terminal pack edits run zero guards
dispatching the auditor is a Task-tool act
dispatching the auditor is a Task-tool act
dispatching the auditor is a Task-tool act
no headless auditor dispatch path exists from Bash inside a session
fail-closed on the recognized dispatch shape: binder path plus checklist evidence required; the evidence bar is textual (dark area: heuristic)
Codex cannot register plugin subagents — the auditor does not exist there
no agent dispatch from a plain terminal
kaizen writer confined to .karta/sme/ and .karta/kaizen.json
kaizen writer confined to .karta/sme/ and .karta/kaizen.json
kaizen writer confined to .karta/sme/ and .karta/kaizen.json
Bash matcher statically parses write/delete targets for confined writers; clear out-of-surface targets and ambiguous commands are blocked (2.24.0)
kaizen's own writes surface as file-tool calls carrying its agent_type
doctrine on Codex: karta registers no kaizen/doc-gardner agent there, so no writer exists for a confinement hook to recognize (docs/how-to/codex.md)
kaizen is an agent construct — there is no terminal kaizen writer
doc-gardner writer confined to the prose-doc surface (README, docs/, AGENTS.md, ARCHITECTURE, .gitignore) — 2.24.0 table row
same surface as docgardner-write:write
same surface as docgardner-write:write
static Bash write/delete parsing, same mechanism as kaizen-write:bash (2.24.0)
doc-gardner's own writes surface as file-tool calls carrying its agent_type
doctrine on Codex: no registered doc-gardner agent exists there for a hook to recognize
doc-gardner is an agent construct — there is no terminal doc-gardner writer

advisory, not a wall: a karta-typed SubagentStop over stranded produced-nothing items is turned back once to name them; generic wave workers are indistinguishable at stop, so full enforcement is impossible by identity — deliberate scope (2.24.0)
refs are git plumbing state under .git/, not a named file-tool surface; packed refs make raw file writes unreliable
same as ref-forge:write
same as ref-forge:write
seeded dark area: git update-ref forging done/accepted refs runs no guard
subagent ref forging lands via Bash
no Codex hooks surface
plain-terminal update-ref runs no guard
a commit is not a file-tool call
a commit is not a file-tool call
a commit is not a file-tool call

enforced by this repo's project-settings commit gate (precommit_gate.py), not the plugin; KARTA_SKIP_GATE=1 is the documented per-command escape — self-serviceable by the gated agent and unaudited (dark area: escape hatch); detection is textual, so aliases and script-internal commits run zero gates
subagent commits land via Bash
no commit gate on Codex — .codex/rules/karta.rules covers git push only
plain-terminal commits run zero gates — no CI backstop
a merge is not a file-tool call
a merge is not a file-tool call
a merge is not a file-tool call

merge-time has no matcher; guard_delivery_stop.py nudges once per (session, state) at session Stop — advisory block-once, fail-open (dark area: purely advisory in several real states) — so the mutation-time cell stays an unwaivered bypass
subagent merges land via Bash
Codex ships a Stop surface and the 2.24.0 bundle carries a Stop-gate twin, trust-gated per hook — until reviewed and trusted, doctrine only
plain-terminal merges run zero guards
